task: Implemented dual-voice 'sonic logo' for session-done notifications and added voice parameter support to the speak() function.
discussion_summary: The askr voice notification system has been extended to support per-call voice selection and a two-voice signature pattern for the session-done ping. The 'Done.' prefix now plays in one voice (askr's branded prefix voice) followed by the detail in a second, distinct voice, creating a recognizable sonic logo that differentiates askr's notifications from generic TTS. The implementation refactored preconditions into a shared gate, added voice parameter to speak(), introduced speak_signature() for the two-tone pattern, and updated the done-ping logic to use random phrases when no goals are completed. All 161 tests pass.

accomplishments.what: Added voice parameter to speak(text, voice='') to support per-call voice selection via macOS -v flag
Implemented speak_signature(prefix, body, prefix_voice, body_voice) for two-voice sequential playback
Refactored voice preconditions into shared _say_preconditions() gate used by both speak() and speak_signature()
Updated _speak_session_done() to use speak_signature() with configurable prefix and body voices
Added _GENERIC_DONE_PHRASES rotation for no-goal case to vary the detail portion of the done ping
Added test coverage for speak_signature() and voice parameter in test_voice.py
Verified live playback through actual code path with real macOS say command

next_actions.action: Commit the voice.py and stop.py changes with message 'feat(voice): dual-voice sonic logo for session-done ping'
next_actions.why: Changes are complete, tested, and verified live; ready for permanent record

next_actions.action: Verify that load_voice_prefix() and load_voice_body() config keys exist in askr/state/config.py or add them if missing
next_actions.why: The stop.py code references these config loaders; they must be present for the feature to work end-to-end

decisions.decision: Two-voice pattern uses sequential subprocess.run() calls rather than a single say command with multiple -v flags
decisions.reason: macOS say does not support switching voices mid-utterance; sequential calls with brief natural pause between them create the desired effect

decisions.decision: Preconditions gate (voice_notifications on, macOS, say on PATH) is shared via _say_preconditions() rather than duplicated
decisions.reason: Single source of truth for the gating logic; both speak() and speak_signature() must pass the same checks

decisions.decision: speak_signature() always fires both prefix and body if preconditions pass, even if one is empty string
decisions.reason: Allows caller to control whether prefix is spoken; empty string is a valid 'skip this part' signal

decisions.decision: Generic done phrases are rotated randomly rather than cycling through a fixed sequence
decisions.reason: Prevents predictable repetition; user hears variety across multiple sessions

relational_files.why: Provides load_voice_enabled(), load_voice_prefix(), load_voice_body() config loaders used by voice.py and stop.py
Contains 5 new test cases for speak_signature() and voice parameter; all 31 voice tests pass
